Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

Please remove sanity-sec test_51 from the always_except list, otherwise this code will not be tested properly.

Patch Set 1:

The ll_vfs_getxattr() function is also used by PCC on the client. It isn't clear if this change to remove all the permission checking is safe or not. 

Yingjin, has the client already done all of the access control checks before calling ll_vfs_getxattr() in pcc_try_dataset_attach()?  I think this change is ok, because this call os only getting the file layout on the PCC object, so that should always be safe.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

I'm not sure why, but this subtest failed for the only time in 6 months on this patch, please review.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

| has the client already done all of the access control checks before calling ll_vfs_getxattr() in pcc_try_dataset_attach()?
Yes, I think it is safe as we do the access control on all file access paths already.
ll_vfs_getattr() is only used to get the layout gen to verify that PCC is valid cached on PCC.

> I'm not sure why, but this subtest failed for the only time in 6 months on this patch, please review.

It looks like it is not related to this patch.
I created LU-18138 for it. ".lustre" is readder-ed when it is alone in last directory block.

parents.subject: LU-18086 obdclass: do not use seeks to store pool order

commit.subject: LU-18088 osd: use __vfs_getxattr
commit.message:
LU-18088 osd: use __vfs_getxattr

vfs_getxattr() is not suitable for osd. To get security xattr it goes
via:

vfs_getxattr
  xattr_getsecurity
    security_inode_getsecurity
      cap_inode_getsecurity
        dentry = d_find_any_alias(inode)
          if (!dentry)
            return -EINVAL;
and return -EINVAL because osd object inodes have empty alias list.

Use __vfs_getxattr() instead.

Fixes: b9ef5d1e7f7 ("LU-17546 osd: use __vfs_removexattr")
HPE-bug-id: LUS-12018
Signed-off-by: Vladimir Saveliev <vladimir.saveliev@hpe.com>
